issue_title: WRC Generations – The FIA WRC Official Game (1953520)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: WRC Generations
- Steam AppID of the game: 1953520

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1080Ti
- Driver/LLVM version: 520.56.06
- Kernel version: 6.0.6
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):
- Proton version: Latest experimental, latest hotfix and 7.0-4.

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[log.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9936522/log.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
The game says "Your system does not support the DirectX Agilty SDK"
## Reproduction
Start the game and you'll get the following message:
![Screenshot_20221104_014431](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9913684/199930657-e4406aaa-9f96-4395-af98-51004ac52fcb.png)

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Animaze by FaceRig
- Steam AppID of the game: 1364390

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX Vega 56/64
- Driver/LLVM version: X.Org AMD VEGA10 (DRM 3.35.0, 5.4.0-65-generic, LLVM 11.0.0)
- Kernel version: 5.4.0-65-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [here](https://gist.github.com/redyoshi49q/2e188665a4e9581c5cdf871bd5979615) (The laptop is an Acer Predator Helios 500 with AMD hardware.)
- Proton version: 5.13-5 (and others; see Reproduction section)

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and drag
and drop the generated `$HOME/steam-$APPID.log` into this issue report -->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When attempting to launch Animaze from Steam, Steam fails to complete the first time setup, and consequently, the program never launches.  The dialog box will alternate between displaying "Installing:  (step 1 of 0)" and "Installing:  (step 1 of 5)".  Animaze's startup scripts seem to attempt to install virtual device drivers; it seems plausible that this is causing Steam's attempt to launch Animaze to hang.

Note that Steam did not generate a ~/steam-1365390.log file as expected, even with `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` in the launch options for the title.  As such, no such file is attached here.

## Reproduction
This behavior reproduces consistently on attempted launch of the application from Steam.  Proton versions tested include 5.13-5, Proton Experimental (as of February 12, 2021), and third party version 6.0-GE-1.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: NIGHT-RUNNERS™ PROLOGUE
- Steam AppID of the game: 2707900

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6900 XT
- Video driver version: Mesa 24.0.2-arch1.2
- Kernel version: 6.7.9-arch1-1
- Link to full system information report as Gist: https://gist.github.com/ClaudeLib/5eba8a8f2aa039d7dc835d07cab06006
- Proton version: 8.0-5, 9.0 Beta and Experimental.

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-2707900-8-amdvlk.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14551162/steam-2707900-8-amdvlk.log)
[steam-2707900-8-radv.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14551163/steam-2707900-8-radv.log)
[steam-2707900-9-amdvlk.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14551164/steam-2707900-9-amdvlk.log)
[steam-2707900-9-radv.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14551165/steam-2707900-9-radv.log)
[steam-2707900-e-amdvlk.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14551166/steam-2707900-e-amdvlk.log)
[steam-2707900-e-radv.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14551167/steam-2707900-e-radv.log)

## Symptoms
The game starts fine, but instead of the intro video I only see the test pattern, which softlocks the game.
![20240310213855_1](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/5920490/99e25e8d-cffb-4832-b668-98d5f73e178f)
By default Steam uses the open-source AMD driver (the Proton logs with it have `amdvlk` in the name), however I also installed the RADV driver `vulkan-radeon lib32-vulkan-radeon` and invoked it by adding `VK_ICD_FILENAMES=/usr/share/vulkan/icd.d/radeon_icd.x86_64.json` to the launch parameters (those logs have `radv` in the name). I tried three versions of Proton, 8.0-5 (`8` in the log name), 9.0 Beta (`9`) and Experimental (`e`), the result is the same across all six combinations.
However, the game seems to run fine on Steam Deck, including video playback: [steam-2707900-deck.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14551168/steam-2707900-deck.log)

## Reproduction
Just run the game, I guess.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Bright Memory: Infinite
- Steam AppID of the game: 1178830

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 5700 
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.3.2 - kisak-mesa PPA
- Kernel version:  5.13.0-22-generic
- Proton version:  proton-6.3-8

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

Can confirm that the game is no longer playable with recent versions, tried with Proton 6.3-8 and Experimental (and also Proton-GE)

Game gets to the title screen, and when hitting enter it hangs on a white screen. With older Proton version 5.13-6 the game is playable, but is missing the videos.
[logs.zip](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7766208/logs.zip)
[system_info.txt](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7766211/system_info.txt)

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: System Shock
- Steam AppID of the game: 482400

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6700XT
- Driver/LLVM version: 4.6 Mesa 23.2.0-devel (git-bf97afa2ab)
- Kernel version: 6.3
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/NoXPhasma/5ec8ffca4aa0c3ebab1404849d131da6
- Proton version: Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

Log file with DX11: [steam-482400.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/11643135/steam-482400.log)
Log file with DX12: [steam-482400_DX12.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/11643136/steam-482400_DX12.log)

The help text on the map is distorted, and it's impossible to read what it says, here's a screenshot on how it looks:
![20230603174743_1](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/7719590/b84a21b5-c453-4c03-9352-f4f7ff856123)
And here's a screenshot I found online on how it should look:
![grafik](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/7719590/c34a77ac-c720-4142-9fc3-42a720aedbe1)

I've tested with RADV and amdvlk, both are affected.


## Reproduction


- Start the game
- open the map
- hover over the map until a help text shows up

issue_title: The Last of Us™ Part II Remastered (2531310)
compatibility_report:
The last of us part 2 remastered steam sond problems

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/8864.
@Frelidon posted on 2025-06-28T21:10:20:

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: The Last of Us Part II remastered
- Steam AppID of the game: 2531310

## System Information
- GPU: < RX 7800XT >
- Video driver version: < 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 25.1.4 >
- Kernel version: < 6.15.3-201.nobara.fc42.x86_64 (64-bit) >
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):
- Proton version: Proton Hotfix

## I confirm:
- [Yes ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [Yes ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Hi, I have a problem with the sound scratching in the last of us part 2 remastered steam. I found this fix on Reddit and it helps.  

pw-metadata -n settings 0 clock.force-rate 48000 && pw-metadata -n settings 0 clock.force-quantum 500

https://www.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/1htxdig/audio_crackles_nobara_41/?tl=de

Unfortunately, I don't know if the operating system or Proton is causing the error. I've also started an article on Nobara.

https://github.com/Nobara-Project/nobara-core-packages/issues/60

Gist: 
https://gist.github.com/Frelidon/287ab146ce26d3f1d26d4377e0050f59

attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Game was running fine last week
Currently game does not launch. Clicking 'Play' shows 'syncing with steam cloud' and then nothing.  So far have tried:
- verify game integrity
- redownload game
- clear download cache
- update system
- reinstall steam
- delete ~/.steam and ~/.local/share/Steam folders, and reinstall

Adding `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to launch options does not create a file in my home folder.  The steam stdout is as follows when launching game:

